text.title: Linear Workspace and Team Configuration
text.summary: Team configuration, issue properties, cycle workflow, project and roadmap setup for engineering teams.

rule.statement: Configure each team with: key prefix (e.g., BAK), workflow states (Backlog/Todo/In Progress/In Review/Done/Canceled), Fibonacci estimate scale (0/1/2/3/5/8/13), and triage enabled.
rule.rationale: Team key prefixes make issue identifiers human-readable across multi-team workspaces (BAK-123 vs FE-456); Fibonacci scale prevents false precision in estimates.

rule.statement: Set cycle duration to 2 weeks, start day Monday, auto-archive enabled — cycles provide time-boxing without requiring manual sprint management.
rule.rationale: Linear cycles auto-advance and archive completed issues; manual sprint management adds overhead that Linear's opinionated workflow is designed to eliminate.

rule.statement: Required issue properties: Status, Priority (Urgent/High/Medium/Low/No Priority), Estimate (Fibonacci), Cycle, Project, Label, Assignee. Priority is an integer enum (Urgent=1, High=2, Medium=3, Low=4, NoPriority=0) in the API — not a string.
rule.rationale: Agents receiving natural-language priority must map explicitly to integers before calling the API; passing strings causes silent mutation failure.

rule.statement: Cycle scope must be frozen after Day 2 of the cycle; API-driven cycle assignment must target nextCycle or futureCycle only — never the active cycle.
rule.rationale: Mid-cycle additions disrupt velocity tracking and flow; agents that auto-add issues to active cycles create scope creep without human intent.

rule.statement: Cycle IDs rotate every cycle; always resolve the current cycle by querying team.activeCycle — never hardcode a cycle ID in agent configuration.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded cycle IDs break every 2 weeks when the cycle advances; dynamic resolution via activeCycle query is always correct.

rule.statement: Projects span multiple cycles and represent initiatives; use milestones within projects to mark key deliverables with target dates.
rule.rationale: Cycles are time-boxes; projects are outcome-scopes. Mixing them confuses velocity (cycle) with delivery (project completion).
